team_context.cap_situation: tight — were $16M over the cap entering the 2026 league year, have restructured multiple deals (Mekari, Murray, Lewis, Hainsey) to get barely compliant. ~$4M in space after restructures. Expect post-June 1 cuts (Armstead) to create more room.
team_context.trade_posture: Competing team coming off 13-4 season and AFC South title. Buyers at DT, CB depth, and potentially LB after losing Devin Lloyd in free agency. Could move overpaid veterans (Armstead, Hamilton) or redundant OL (Walker Little already trade candidate) for picks. No first-round pick in 2026 (traded for Travis Hunter at #2 overall in 2025).

players.name: Trevor Lawrence
players.performance_evidence: Franchise-record 38 total TDs (29 passing, 9 rushing) in 2025. 4,007 passing yards, 84.7 PFF overall grade (8th among QBs). Led Jaguars to 13-4 record and AFC South title. Emerged as MVP candidate in second half with 19 TDs and 7 INTs during 8-game win streak. Massive improvement over 2024 injury-shortened season.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old franchise QB on the upswing with 5 years of control. Even with the massive contract ($55M AAV), his age and trajectory make him essentially untradeable. The $116.5M dead cap makes any trade financially impossible. Contract includes a full no-trade clause.

players.availability_reasoning: Full no-trade clause, $116.5M dead cap, franchise QB who just led team to a division title and is only 26. Jaguars hired Liam Coen specifically to develop Lawrence. Zero chance of a trade.
players.injury_notes: Missed 7 games in 2024 with left shoulder injury (surgery December 2024) and concussion. Played all 17 games healthy in 2025.
players.trade_buzz: Pittsburgh inquired about Lawrence before 2025 season per reports, but Jaguars emphatically shut it down.

players.name: Nick Mullens
players.performance_evidence: Career backup with experience in multiple systems. Provides capable insurance behind Lawrence. Did not see meaningful action in 2025 given Lawrence's health.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value — teams can find similar options in free agency. Worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Backup QB with no long-term commitment. Team would move him if they found a better option, but not actively looking to trade.

players.name: Bhayshul Tuten
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: 83 carries, 307 yards, 5 rushing TDs plus 10 receptions for 79 yards and 2 TDs. Led rookie RBs in kickoff return average (28.4 yds). Flashed explosiveness (career-high 15 carries, 74 yards vs Chargers). Now elevated to RB1 after Travis Etienne departed to Saints in free agency.

trade_value.reasoning: Young RB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control, but unproven as a feature back. RB position value is low. Worth a late 4th/early 5th based on potential and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: With Etienne gone, Tuten is the presumptive RB1. Team needs him in the backfield. Would only move for a clear overpay given the thin RB room.
players.injury_notes: Missed time with finger injury requiring surgery in 2025 (Weeks 13-17 limited).

players.name: LeQuint Allen Jr.
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who earned a role as pass-blocking specialist and third-down back. Limited touches but praised by coaches for dirty work. Forced a fumble in Wild Card game vs Bills. Fan and coaching staff favorite for his old-school mentality.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA RB with minimal production data. On a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value — a 7th-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth RB on a minimum deal. Team would move him for any reasonable pick but he's also not being shopped.

players.name: Brian Thomas Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl as a rookie in 2024 (87 receptions, 1,282 yards, 10 TDs). Struggled significantly in Year 2: 48 receptions, 707 yards, 2 TDs in 2025 with a 65.5 PFF grade (59th among WRs). Dealt with drops and inconsistency that prompted the Meyers trade. Still only 23 with elite physical tools — team expects growth from tough Year 2.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round WR who was a Pro Bowl rookie, still only 23 on a cheap deal with 3 years of control. The 2025 regression is concerning but his age, pedigree, and contract make him extremely valuable. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Team is 'leaving the light on' for Thomas per reporting. They believe in his talent and expect a bounce-back. Would only trade for a massive haul, but the disappointing Year 2 means he's not untouchable.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN's Bill Barnwell floated a Thomas for Keon Coleman swap in a hypothetical trade piece, suggesting some analysts see Thomas as potentially available.

players.name: Jakobi Meyers
players.performance_evidence: Acquired from Raiders midseason, immediately transformed the offense. 27 catches, 355 yards, 3 TDs in 6 games with Jaguars. Team went 5-1 after his arrival. Career: 453 catches, 5,299 yards, never more than 2 drops in a season. Led NFL with 8 catches on 11 targets on 'in' routes during 5-game win streak. $30M fully guaranteed at signing, $40M total guaranteed.

trade_value.reasoning: Reliable possession WR2 on a new 3-year/$60M deal. The contract is market-rate but not team-friendly. At 29, he's entering his decline window. The $21M dead cap makes a 2026 trade virtually impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year/$60M extension in December 2025. $21M dead cap makes him untradeable in 2026. He's the reason the offense took off in the second half. Team views him as essential to Lawrence's development.

players.name: Parker Washington
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season — had a punt return TD (87 yards vs 49ers), became a reliable WR3. Extension candidate alongside Brenton Strange. 6th-round pick who earned a real role through development and special teams impact.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old WR3 entering final year of rookie deal. Cheap and productive but only 1 year of control remaining. Worth a mid-to-late 4th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Extension candidate this offseason per beat writers. Team values his development and special teams impact. Would need a strong offer to pry him loose given the WR room already lost depth.

players.name: Brenton Strange
players.performance_evidence: Career-best 46 receptions, 540 yards in 2025. Spotrac projects his market value at ~4 years/$48M ($12M/yr). Entering contract year in 2026. Second-round pedigree with steady development arc.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old TE on the final year of his rookie deal at just $2M cap hit. Ascending player with extension potential. The one year of control limits value, but his age and trajectory make him worth a 3rd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Extension candidate this offseason. Team wants to keep him long-term but if contract talks stall, they could trade him rather than lose him for nothing. Not being actively shopped.

players.name: Quintin Morris
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed before free agency 2026. Valued for pass-blocking ability. Started on practice squad in 2025 but played in 14 games with 5 starts. Key contributor in run game and on special teams.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE2 on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value — a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed but on a cheap deal. Team values his blocking. Would trade for anything reasonable but not being shopped.

players.name: Cole Van Lanen
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: started 9 games at 4 different OL positions, locked down LT in the final stretch. Got a 3-year/$51M extension ($32.5M guaranteed) based on performance. Small sample size at LT but impressed enough for a mid-market deal. PFF grades were solid when starting.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old LT on a team-friendly cap hit in Year 1 ($5.2M). But the $23.8M dead cap makes a trade impossible in 2026. Contract is a bet on his continued development.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year/$51M extension in January 2026. $23.8M dead cap makes him completely untradeable. Team invested heavily in him as their LT of the future.

players.name: Ezra Cleveland
players.performance_evidence: Solid performances at LG in 2025 after injuries limited him previously. Listed as restructure or potential cap casualty candidate for 2026, but 'probably a lock to return' per beat writers due to solid play. Former 2nd-round pick acquired via trade from Cleveland in 2023.

trade_value.reasoning: Decent starting guard but on an expiring deal with a $10.5M cap hit and $7.4M dead cap if traded. The dead cap significantly limits savings from a trade. Worth a 4th-5th round pick but the cap math makes it complicated.

players.availability_reasoning: Mentioned as restructure candidate or possible cap casualty. Contract expires after 2026. Team would trade if the right offer came, especially with Wyatt Milum developing behind him.
players.injury_notes: Battled injuries in 2024 that limited effectiveness.
players.trade_buzz: Named as restructure candidate by multiple beat reporters. Could be moved if Milum is ready.

players.name: Robert Hainsey
players.performance_evidence: Signed as UFA from Tampa Bay in 2025. Provided stability at center. Just had his contract restructured (saved $4M in cap space), indicating team commitment. Part of the OL group that helped Lawrence have his best season.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old starting center on a reasonable deal with 2 years remaining. Centers aren't premium trade assets. Worth a mid-4th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just restructured his deal, showing team commitment. Starting center on a competing team. Would need an overpay to move.
players.injury_notes: Missed some time in 2025; Jonah Monheim filled in capably.

players.name: Patrick Mekari
players.performance_evidence: Signed from Baltimore as UFA in 2025. 'Less consistent at right guard' per beat writers in first year switching sides. Recently had contract restructured (saved $7.4M). Extremely versatile — can play all 5 OL positions. Team showing confidence by restructuring rather than cutting.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile OL who can play all 5 positions, but below-average as a starting guard. 2-year deal with modest dead cap. Worth a late 4th/early 5th for his versatility alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Restructured deal shows commitment for now, but his versatility makes him a trade asset if young OL (Milum) develop. Not being actively shopped but available at the right price.

players.name: Anton Harrison
players.performance_evidence: Career-best PFF overall grade of 72.9 in 2025. 76.7 PFF pass-blocking grade (20th in NFL), allowed just 1 sack on 643 pass-blocking snaps. 5.1% pressure rate since 2023 is 22nd-lowest among 104 tackles. Started 15 games in 2025. Fifth-year option ($19.956M) expected to be exercised — 'no-brainer' per beat writers and NFL analysts.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old starting RT on a cheap rookie deal, improving every year. Fifth-year option gives team 2 years of control. At RT, he's a franchise-caliber player in the making. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd given age, position, and ascending trajectory.

players.availability_reasoning: Ascending 23-year-old RT on a cheap deal with the 5th-year option about to be exercised. One of the most important pieces of the OL that helped Lawrence's breakout. Team views him as a cornerstone.
players.injury_notes: Missed 2 games in 2025 but otherwise durable (started 48 of 49 career games).

players.name: Josh Hines-Allen
players.performance_evidence: Franchise all-time sack leader (61 career sacks). 8 sacks in 2025 (plus 1 in playoffs), 86.8 PFF overall grade (6th among EDGE), top 10 in pressures (80) and QB hits (26). Broke franchise career sack record vs Chargers in Week 11. Had 17.5 sacks in 2023 (franchise single-season record). Two-time Pro Bowler. $141.25M deal signed April 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite-tier EDGE rusher, franchise icon, 29 years old with 4 years remaining. Still producing at a high level (86.8 PFF grade). The massive $45M dead cap makes any trade impossible. Worth a high first-round pick on talent alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise icon, all-time sack leader, $45M dead cap if traded. Recently signed to a 5-year/$141.25M extension. Team needs to restructure his deal for cap relief, not trade him. Zero chance.
players.injury_notes: Placed on IR with knee injury in 2020. Fully healthy 2021-2025, playing all 17 games each of the last 3 seasons.

players.name: DaVon Hamilton
players.performance_evidence: Made a key play in OT win vs Raiders (swatted ball at LOS). Part of the NFL's #1 run defense in 2025 (85.6 yards/game allowed). On a 3-year/$34.5M deal signed in 2023. Cap hit of $12.4M in final year. Listed as cap casualty candidate by multiple analysts alongside Armstead. Releasing after June 1 saves $8.4M.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starting DT on an expiring deal with a $12.4M cap hit. Provides some value as a run stuffer but is a cap casualty candidate. Worth a late 5th/6th round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cap casualty candidate per multiple analysts. $12.4M cap hit in final year of deal. Jaguars would save $8.4M by cutting him post-June 1. If they can get a mid-round pick instead of cutting him, they'd jump at it.
players.trade_buzz: Named as cap casualty candidate by Bleacher Report and multiple outlets alongside Armstead.

players.name: Arik Armstead
players.performance_evidence: 5.5 sacks in 2025 (best since 2021) but 63.0 PFF defensive grade ('extremely average'). Started strong but faded after hand injury mid-season. $19.4M cap hit on a 3-year/$43.5M deal. Top cap casualty candidate — post-June 1 cut saves $14.5M. Pre-June 1 cut saves only $2.3M making an early-offseason trade nearly impossible.

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old DT with a bloated $19.4M cap hit and $17.1M dead cap if traded pre-June 1. The dead cap exceeds the cap hit, making a pre-June 1 trade a negative for the Jaguars. Post-June 1 release is the likely path. Essentially negative trade value due to contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Top cap casualty candidate per every Jaguars beat writer and national analyst. The only question is whether it's a restructure, post-June 1 cut, or post-June 1 trade. The $17.1M dead cap makes a pre-March trade impossible, but a post-June 1 trade could work if a team takes on his $14M base salary.
players.injury_notes: Hand injury in 2025 that hampered second-half production.
players.trade_buzz: Consistently named as the Jaguars' top cap casualty candidate by NFL.com, Bleacher Report, Black and Teal, and SI.

players.name: Travon Walker
players.performance_evidence: Back-to-back 10+ sack seasons in 2023-2024 (10.0 and 10.5 sacks). Disappointing 2025: only 3.5 sacks due to wrist injury (Week 4, required surgery, played with club) and knee injury. Still just 24 years old. Top run defender (9.4% run-stop rate, 9th among EDGE). On fully guaranteed $15.2M 5th-year option. Extension candidate — comparable to George Karlaftis ($93M/4yr).

trade_value.reasoning: Former #1 overall pick, only 24, with two double-digit sack seasons on his resume. The 2025 injury year depressed his value, but his upside is enormous. On a fully guaranteed 5th-year option so cap hit equals dead cap — no savings from trading. Extension could create cap relief. Worth a late 1st based on talent/age alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Extension candidate this offseason. Team could 'buy low' after injury-depressed 2025 season. If extension talks fail, he becomes a 2027 free agent and his trade value increases during the 2026 season. The fully guaranteed 5th-year option means no cap savings from a trade, making it unlikely unless a team overpays.
players.injury_notes: Wrist surgery after Week 4 in 2025, played with club rest of season. Also missed 2 games with knee injury. Had back-to-back fully healthy 17-game seasons in 2023-2024.
players.trade_buzz: Extension negotiations ongoing. Beat reporters suggest Jaguars may try to lock him up at a below-market rate while value is depressed from injury year. Comparable to Karlaftis deal (~$23M/yr).

players.name: Foyesade Oluokun
players.performance_evidence: 134 tackles (65 solo), 1 sack, career-high 10 pass breakups, 1 INT, 2 forced fumbles in 2025. 6th consecutive season with 100+ tackles. Led NFL in tackles twice (2021, 2022). Restructured contract in November 2025. AFC Defensive Player of the Week in Week 1 2025. Named as potential cap cut per NFLTradeRumors given team's Rams-style philosophy of minimal LB investment.

trade_value.reasoning: Prolific tackler on a moderate deal, but 30 years old and LB is a low-value position. Cap hit of ~$12M with $8M dead cap limits savings. Worth a late 3rd/early 4th if team eats some dead cap.

players.availability_reasoning: Named as restructure candidate or potential cap casualty by multiple outlets. With Devin Lloyd gone to Panthers, Oluokun becomes more important — but the team's Rams-influenced philosophy de-prioritizes LB spending. Available at fair value but probably returns in 2026.
players.trade_buzz: Listed as restructure/cap casualty candidate. Team's Rams-philosophy roots suggest they may not want to invest heavily at LB.

players.name: Dennis Gardeck
players.performance_evidence: Just re-signed: 2-year/$6.5M deal ($3.65M guaranteed). 48 tackles, 2.5 sacks, 9 QB hits, 6 TFL, 1 forced fumble in 2025. Also 11 special teams tackles. 19.5 career sacks over 8 NFL seasons. Two-time Pro Bowl alternate. Versatile — plays LB and pass-rusher roles. Came back from ACL tear in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old rotational LB/pass-rusher on a cheap deal. Provides value but is replaceable. Worth a 6th-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a cheap deal. Team values his versatility and special teams. Not being shopped but available for fair value.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in 2024 with Cardinals. Fully recovered and played all 17 games in 2025.

players.name: Jarrian Jones
players.performance_evidence: 78.4 PFF overall grade in 2025 (5th among 114 CBs), 78.7 coverage grade (5th). 49 tackles, 8 pass defenses, 3 INTs in 17 games. Allowed just 58.5% completion rate and 62.2 NFL Rating in coverage. Stepped up massively after Travis Hunter's ACL injury in Week 8. Also had solid rookie year in 2024 (40 tackles, 1 INT, served as top slot CB).

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old CB on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years of control, grading out as a top-5 CB in the NFL. Ascending player at a premium position. Worth a high 2nd-round pick given his production, age, and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Ascending young CB on a cheap deal is extremely valuable. Team needs CB depth especially with Hunter's status uncertain. Would only trade for a massive overpay — think 1st-round pick territory.
players.injury_notes: Battled quadriceps and back issues during 2025 but played through most of them.

players.name: Christian Braswell
players.performance_evidence: 6th-round pick who has developed into a reliable depth/spot starter. 14 tackles, 3 pass defenses in 2025 per team site. Made a key pass breakup in OT win vs Cardinals. With Montaric Brown re-signed, Braswell projects as CB3/CB4 depth behind Hunter, Brown, Jones, and Lewis.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth CB on an expiring rookie deal. Minimal trade value — a conditional 6th at best. More valuable to keep as cheap depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth CB on an expiring deal. Team has Brown, Hunter, Jones, and Lewis ahead of him. Available at fair value but also not being shopped.

players.name: Jourdan Lewis
players.performance_evidence: Veteran nickel corner signed in 2025 free agency. Missed time with injury in 2025 but was a key piece when healthy. Contract just restructured (saved $6.1M in cap), showing team commitment. Projected to man slot alongside Jones in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old nickel CB with 2 years remaining at $7.5M AAV. Dead cap ($5M) exceeds potential cap savings, making a trade counterproductive in 2026. Worth a late 4th on talent but contract structure prevents it.

players.availability_reasoning: Just restructured his deal, meaning team is committed. Dead cap exceeds potential savings. Effectively locked in for 2026.
players.injury_notes: Missed multiple games in 2025 with undisclosed injury. Was inactive for Week 12 (Cardinals).

players.name: Eric Murray
players.performance_evidence: Veteran safety signed in 2025 free agency. Started alongside Antonio Johnson. Contract just restructured ($4.2M of salary converted to signing bonus, saved $3.36M). Beat writers project him to compete with young safeties (Caleb Ransaw) for starting role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old starting safety on a modest deal. Dead cap nearly equals cap hit, making a trade pointless for cap relief. Worth a late 5th/early 6th on talent alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Restructured deal shows some commitment, but beat writers suggest he could lose his starting job to Caleb Ransaw (2025 3rd-round pick returning from injury). If Ransaw steps up, Murray becomes expendable.

players.name: Antonio Johnson
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season. Part of the defense that forced the 2nd-most turnovers in the NFL. Had 5 interceptions (tied for team lead with Devin Lloyd). 5th-round pick who developed into a starting-caliber safety. Cheap final year of rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old ascending safety on a dirt-cheap expiring deal. Only 1 year of control limits value, but production and age suggest a team could extend him. Worth a 3rd-round pick given his breakout year.

players.availability_reasoning: Ascending 24-year-old safety on a cheap deal. Team needs him, especially with Caleb Ransaw unproven. Extension candidate. Would only trade for a clear overpay.

players.name: Chris Rodriguez Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Physical, downhill runner who served as a change-of-pace/short-yardage complement behind Travis Etienne. Carried roughly 70-90 times in 2024 with modest per-carry production (~3.8 YPC). Limited pass-catching role. Provides value as a between-the-tackles grinder but hasn't shown the burst or versatility to command a larger role. Entered 2025 as the clear RB2 behind Tank Bigsby following Etienne's departure.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round RB2 on a cheap rookie deal but limited production ceiling and minimal standalone trade value. Worth a 6th/7th at best; most teams could find an equivalent back in the draft or on waivers.

players.availability_reasoning: Replaceable rotational RB on a minimum-level contract. Jacksonville would move him for any meaningful draft capital, though there's unlikely to be significant demand. Team could also keep him for depth at virtually no cost.
players.character_notes: Had a DUI arrest at Kentucky in 2022 that led to a 4-game college suspension; it factored into his draft slide to the 5th round. No NFL-level incidents since.

players.name: Ventrell Miller
players.performance_evidence: Former Florida captain who earned a starting role by mid-2024 and held it into 2025. Solid run defender who fits Jacksonville's scheme as a dependable two-down linebacker. Registered roughly 80-90 combined tackles in 2024 with a handful of TFLs. Limited in coverage — not a three-down player. PFF run-defense grades have been above-average while coverage grades lag behind. A functional starter rather than a difference-maker.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting LB on a near-minimum contract. The cost-controlled deal adds some value, but off-ball linebacker is a low-value position and his limited coverage ability caps his worth at roughly a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Day-3 pick who has developed into a serviceable starter, but Jacksonville wouldn't hesitate to move him for a 4th or 5th-round pick given LB depth is relatively easier to replace. Not being actively shopped but not a core building block.
players.injury_notes: Suffered two ACL tears while at Florida (2020 and 2021). Has been healthy throughout his NFL career so far, but the injury history is notable and could affect how acquiring teams view his long-term durability.

players.name: Travis Hunter
players.performance_evidence: 2024 Heisman Trophy winner as a true two-way player at Colorado — elite cornerback and high-level wide receiver. Won the Bednarik, Biletnikoff, and AP Player of the Year in the same season, a historically unprecedented feat. Selected 1st overall by Jacksonville in the 2025 NFL Draft. As a rookie in 2025, Hunter projects (and by this point has likely demonstrated) as a lockdown CB1 with the athleticism, ball skills, and competitiveness to be an immediate shutdown corner. His two-way college usage is unprecedented, and while the NFL role focuses on defense, his ceiling is the best cornerback in football. Length (6'1"), speed (4.32 40), and instincts give him an elite toolkit.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old #1 overall pick on a fully guaranteed 4-year rookie deal with All-Pro CB ceiling. This is the single most valuable non-QB trade asset possible — a young, cost-controlled, potentially generational cornerback. His trade value exceeds any single draft pick; in practice it would take multiple 1sts to pry him loose. Expressed as a pick equivalent he's worth the #1 overall pick or more.

players.availability_reasoning: Selected 1st overall just one draft ago. Franchise cornerstone, face-of-the-franchise potential, on a fully guaranteed rookie contract that is massively team-friendly relative to his projected production. Jacksonville's entire defensive rebuild is anchored around Hunter. The dead cap alone ($36.5M) makes a trade financially impossible, and even if it didn't, no front office trades the #1 pick after one year. There is no realistic offer that moves this player.
